channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA launches rescue mission to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere - Space
item.description:
1. NASA launches rescue mission to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere  Space
  2. Daring rescue mission launches to save a 3,200-pound NASA observatory from an untimely end  CNN
  3. Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope  BBC
  4. Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that’s falling back to Earth  AP News
  5. A Rescue Mission to Save NASA’s Swift Telescope Launches to Orbit  The New York Times

item.title: 'It's more than a hope, it's a guarantee': The Vera C. Rubin Observatory's 10-year movie of the universe is about to 'blow our minds,' chief scientist Tony Tyson says - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'It's more than a hope, it's a guarantee': The Vera C. Rubin Observatory's 10-year movie of the universe is about to 'blow our minds,' chief scientist Tony Tyson says  Live Science
  2. ‘The greatest cosmic movie ever made’: Historic telescope kicks off an unprecedented survey  CNN
  3. A giant telescope goes on a decade-long search for dark matter  The Washington Post
  4. A Dazzling Census of Our Cosmos Begins  The New York Times
  5. LSST begins full operations with key contributions from Japanese researchers and engineers  Phys.org

item.title: 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life - Fox News
item.description:
1. 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life  Fox News
  2. This Cell Feeds, Grows and Reproduces. And It’s Manmade.  The New York Times
  3. Scientists say they have built a basic component of life from scratch  CNN
  4. ‘Beautiful blobs’: synthetic life a step closer as scientists make cells using lab-made DNA  The Guardian
  5. Scientists have built a cell in a lab from the ground up  The Economist

item.title: ‘Strong’ Northern Lights Alert: 26 States May See Aurora On July 4 - Forbes
item.description:
1. ‘Strong’ Northern Lights Alert: 26 States May See Aurora On July 4  Forbes
  2. Sun fires off 10 solar flares in 24 hours as multiple Earth-bound CMEs raise northern lights hopes for July 4 weekend  Space
  3. Northern Lights could dazzle the sky in these states due to solar storm ahead of Fourth of July  FOX Weather
  4. Northern lights could dazzle to start July 4 weekend  The Hill
  5. Where to see the northern lights tonight: Aurora borealis forecast  Fast Company

item.title: James Webb telescope may have discovered a never-before-seen substance on Pluto and Titan - Yahoo
item.description: James Webb telescope may have discovered a never-before-seen substance on Pluto and Titan  Yahoo

item.title: Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting' - Space
item.description:
1. Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting'  Space
  2. A world just 25 light-years away sits in the narrow zone where liquid water could exist on its surface — and astronomers at UC Irvine have now revised its mass down to just 2.3 times Earth’s, making it look less like a gas-shrouded mini-Neptune and more like s  Space Daily
  3. UC Irvine astronomers discover a new Earth-like exoplanet  UC Irvine News
  4. Potentially Habitable Super-Earth Found Just 25 Light-Years Away  ScienceAlert
  5. Nearby 'Super Earth' may be a better candidate for life than previously thought  Phys.org

item.title: A Cold Atlantic Anomaly Returns as Super El Niño Shapes Winter 2026/2027 Early Forecast Signals - Severe Weather Europe
item.description:
1. A Cold Atlantic Anomaly Returns as Super El Niño Shapes Winter 2026/2027 Early Forecast Signals  Severe Weather Europe
  2. As the Ocean Warms, a 'Cold Blob' in the Atlantic Has Puzzled Scientists. It Might Be a Warning Sign About a Key Current System  Smithsonian Magazine
  3. Slowdown of AMOC ocean current may be gradual and reversible  New Scientist
  4. Scientists Just Found Something Very Disturbing About the AMOC Current Deep Below the Ocean: Evidence That Its Weakening Isn't Just a Fluke, and If It Collapses, the World Could Be Plunged Into Climate Catastrophe  Futurism
  5. An unusual ‘cold blob’ in the Atlantic is a warning sign for our future weather, researchers say  The Boston Globe

item.title: A tiny “quasi-moon” called Kamoʻoalewa shadows Earth’s orbit and may be a piece of the actual Moon blasted into space by an ancient impact — and China’s Tianwen-2 spacecraft is now closing in to bring a sample back to Earth. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A tiny “quasi-moon” called Kamoʻoalewa shadows Earth’s orbit and may be a piece of the actual Moon blasted into space by an ancient impact — and China’s Tianwen-2 spacecraft is now closing in to bring a sample back to Earth.  Space Daily
  2. Could this asteroid be a piece of the Moon? A Chinese spacecraft is about to find out  The Conversation
  3. China’s Tianwen-2 mission has (probably) arrived at a quasi-moon of Earth  The Planetary Society
  4. China's Tianwen-2 Probe Has Likely Reached Earth's "Quasi-moon" Kamo‘oalewa. Now The Fun Begins  IFLScience
  5. China Is About To Land On Earth’s ‘Mini-Moon’ — What To Know  Forbes

item.title: Scientists uncover why Antarctica became engulfed by ice millions of years before the Arctic - Phys.org
item.description: Scientists uncover why Antarctica became engulfed by ice millions of years before the Arctic  Phys.orgSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star - Space
item.description:
1. 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star  Space

  3. ‘Bizarre’ planet orbiting dead star may preview fate of our solar system  CNN
  4. The sun’s violent death could look like this  Popular Science
  5. This Giant Planet Survived the Death of its Star  Universe Today

item.title: Sea level doesn’t rise at the same rate everywhere – we mapped where Antarctica’s ice melt would have the biggest impact - Yahoo News UK
item.description:
1. Sea level doesn’t rise at the same rate everywhere – we mapped where Antarctica’s ice melt would have the biggest impact  Yahoo News UK

  3. Scientists Sound the Alarm: Antarctic Discovery Is More Disturbing Than Anyone Feared  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  4. Twice a day the tide lifts a glacier the size of Florida, and satellites just caught warm seawater slipping through the door it opens in a way no one thought was possible  ecoportal.net

  5. Learn about meteors at Manfred Olson Planetarium's July 'Shooting Stars' program  WUWM

item.title: Fossil jaws hidden inside Cretaceous rocks have revealed that 100 million years ago, some octopuses grew into giant predators — possibly longer than a bus and more fearsome than any octopus alive today. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Fossil jaws hidden inside Cretaceous rocks have revealed that 100 million years ago, some octopuses grew into giant predators — possibly longer than a bus and more fearsome than any octopus alive today.  Space Daily
  2. Giant school bus–sized octopuses may have once ruled the oceans  Science News Explores
  3. Scientists uncover terrifying 19-meter sea monster that once ruled ancient oceans  Futura, le média qui explore le monde

item.title: Mountain lions moved into a tiny Californian preserve. What happened next astounded scientists - BBC Wildlife Magazine
item.description:
1. Mountain lions moved into a tiny Californian preserve. What happened next astounded scientists  BBC Wildlife Magazine
  2. Mountain lions changed everything in this tiny California preserve  ScienceDaily
  3. When mountain lions began appearing more often on trail cameras at a small California nature preserve, coyotes and deer shifted away from nighttime activity, and the woody plants those deer used to eat increased sixty four fold  Space Daily
  4. One mountain lion changed the food web in a California suburb, study finds  news - Mongabay
  5. Mountain lions left this suburb a century ago; one returned to Stanford's preserve in 2015, and woody plan  The Economic Times

item.title: Scientists discover giant fan-shaped structure beneath Antarctica's ice sheet: The hidden structure rewri - The Times of India
item.description:
1. Scientists discover giant fan-shaped structure beneath Antarctica's ice sheet: The hidden structure rewri  The Times of India
  2. Scientists have detected a giant fan-shaped structure buried beneath the East Antarctic Ice Sheet, suggesting the frozen continent may be hiding the geological scars of the breakup that helped separate Antarctica from Australia  Space Daily
  3. Hidden life beneath Antarctica's dry valleys: Scientists discover alien-like ecosystems that could reshape how we protect Earth  The Times of India
  4. Scientists Stunned by Discovery of Massive Structure Hidden Beneath Antarctica  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  5. A discovery under 2 miles of ice in Antarctica could help solve a geological mystery millions of years old  The Economic Times

item.title: A galaxy confirmed this January formed just 290 million years after the Big Bang, and the part astronomers cannot explain is that it is far too large, far too soon, as if the early universe was building things it was not supposed to be able to build yet. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A galaxy confirmed this January formed just 290 million years after the Big Bang, and the part astronomers cannot explain is that it is far too large, far too soon, as if the early universe was building things it was not supposed to be able to build yet.  Space Daily
  2. The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw  Space
  3. The most distant lensing cluster reveals a dark matter surprise  Big Think
  4. James Webb Space Telescope catches 6 galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe  Yahoo
  5. The JWST Spies Six Galaxies Becoming One  Universe Today

item.title: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara may be a surviving shard of a lost Moon-sized world — one that formed beside the young Sun, followed a path unlike Earth or Mars, and was smashed apart while the planets were still being built. - Space Daily

item.title: In November, a spacecraft that has spent eight years falling toward the Sun will finally settle into orbit around Mercury, a planet we have seen up close fewer times than almost any other in the solar system. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In November, a spacecraft that has spent eight years falling toward the Sun will finally settle into orbit around Mercury, a planet we have seen up close fewer times than almost any other in the solar system.  Space Daily

  3. End of the blue glow: BepiColombo turns off solar electric propulsion for Mercury arrival  European Space Agency
  4. Electric blue thrusters propelling BepiColombo to Mercury  European Space Agency
  5. End of BepiColombo's solar electric propulsion  European Space Agency

item.title: Demographers estimate that about 117 billion members of our species have ever been born, which means the roughly 8 billion of us alive today are about 7% of everyone who has ever lived - Space Daily

item.title: In 2024, engineers rescued Voyager 1 after it spent months transmitting gibberish, remotely rewriting and relocating pieces of 46-year-old code around a failed memory chip from more than 24 billion kilometres away. - Space Daily

item.title: Voyager 1 launched in 1977 on a four-year mission, and in November 2026, nearly half a century later, it will become the first object ever to reach one light-day from Earth — so distant that a signal now takes a full 24 hours to cross the emptiness back home - 19FortyFive
item.description:
1. Voyager 1 launched in 1977 on a four-year mission, and in November 2026, nearly half a century later, it will become the first object ever to reach one light-day from Earth — so distant that a signal now takes a full 24 hours to cross the emptiness back home  19FortyFive
  2. Voyager 1 to reach 1 light-day from Earth on November 18  EarthSky
  3. Voyager 1 isn't dying because anything broke — after almost 50 years it's simply running out of power, and NASA is shutting off its instruments one by one until the signal goes silent.  19FortyFive
  4. A voyage to infinity and beyond: Voyager's remarkable journey continues  Vision IAS
  5. Why was the Voyager 1 spacecraft in the news? - Ask Your Science Teacher  La Crosse Tribune
